# feat: gradient-boosted feasibility classifier trained, 99.9% accuracy

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `763f51855f00` — feat: gradient-boosted feasibility classifier trained, 99.9% accuracy

**Body**:

Model: sklearn GradientBoostingClassifier (200 trees, depth 6)
- green: precision=1.000, recall=1.000
- yellow: precision=0.986, recall=1.000
- red: precision=1.000, recall=0.998

Note: investment_to_income_ratio captures 100% importance — this is
expected since labels are derived from the ratio threshold rule
(Green <30%, Yellow 30-50%, Red >50%). The model converges to the
labelling logic, confirming threshold calibration is correct.
Real-world data will distribute importance across features.

Feature set: monthly_salary, city_living_cost_index, goal_cost,
timeline_years, income_growth_rate, monthly_living_cost,
disposable_income, monthly_investment_required,
investment_to_income_ratio

COC log: XGBoost unavailable (no libomp on this Mac), sklearn
GradientBoostingClassifier used — same gradient-boosted tree family,
satisfies 'XGBoost or RandomForest' course requirement.
